title: Age Gap Relationships & The Science Behind Them
text: hers berated the veteran actor begging him to find someone closer to his own age even at three years old the chart continues to resurface as commentators keep a keen eye on dicaprio's current relationship with 24 year old camilla marone the reactions to the actors dating habits embody the contentious views surrounding age gap relationships for some they are a source of admiration while for others there is something inherently unsettling about coupling up with a much younger person from demi moore to ashton kutcher to george and amal clooney high profile age gap relationships always set tongues wagging although in 2014 the average age difference in u.s heterosexual relationships was relatively small 2.3 years many couples have a much wider gap in western countries around eight percent of male female couples have an age gap of 10 or more rising to 25 in male male and 15 and female female our views around age gap relationships are shaped by thousands of years of evolutionary psychology and generations of social and cultural norms within the last hundred years economic shifts and increased gender equalities have changed what is considered normal age gap and recent social justice movements have increased scrutiny of power dynamics and mixed age relationships couples with large age gaps often face judgment and while some experts believe that this could be about to change others argue that young people could be becoming more disapproving of mixed age love than ever before in many cultures it is not considered acceptable to fall in love with someone who is much older or younger than you says dr elena tarrone a consultant psychologist and co-founder of the chelsea psychology clinic in london from an evolutionary perspective the drive to have a family can have an impact on who we choose to have a relationship with both from a biological perspective but also in the sense of both parents being alive to raise the child and this also brings up the topic of fertility and the fact that a woman's fertility window is much much smaller than a man's this is just the way that it is this is the way we are biologically it's nothing new here and actually i saw the initial 10 years of marriage people report higher levels of marital satisfaction when their partner is younger than them however over time the marital satisfaction of different age couples declines more than similar aged partners the probability of similar age couples divorcing is also lower despite these factors nudging us towards partners of the same age socioeconomic circumstances
